title: Three-layer observability — component, coordination, emergence
summary: Appendix B.4's observability stack. Component metrics (latency, accuracy of A and B) feed coordination metrics (CQ, CE, CS), which feed emergence metrics (EI, novel capabilities). Operator alerts from any layer trigger corrective action that reduces the very signal that raised them — a balancing loop that keeps the system at target health.
source: Appendix B.4 — Coordination Metrics + Observability Stack (Ternary Monitoring)

nodes.label: Coordination Metrics
nodes.description: Layer 2 — CQ, CE, CS, context sensitivity

nodes.label: Emergence Metrics
nodes.description: Layer 3 — EI, novel capability count, superhuman performance

nodes.label: Operator Alerts
nodes.description: Dashboards, pages, SLO breaches — human-in-the-loop trigger

nodes.label: Corrective Action
nodes.description: Config changes, rollback, model refresh — reduces component degradation

edges.description: Component degradation propagates into coordination quality

identifiability_note: All five nodes correspond to explicit measured signals defined in B.4's code. The loop polarity flips twice (degradation lowers emergence, alert reduces degradation) giving a balancing (homeostatic) loop — the correct signature for a working observability stack.
